determinism_check.caveat: avmp_dynamic_mr05 was the only variant re-run; baselines and avmp_static_mr05 were not (cost). The aggregate.py deterministic_subset now strips time_spent_rebalancing_ns from allocator_specific_median/iqr because the counter is wall-clock dependent and that defeats the operation-counter throttle fix. Every other field (rebalance_count, bytes_migrated_total, current_kv_pool_bytes, current_ssm_pool_bytes, auto_rebalance_skipped_throttle, bytes_wasted_to_alignment_total, current_pressure_state_code, kv/ssm_free_ratio) matches byte-for-byte across primary and rerun.
determinism_check.method: Independent rerun (avmp_dynamic_mr05 variant), compared to primary aggregated_deterministic.json rows filtered to variant_label == avmp_dynamic_mr05
determinism_check.result: byte-identical (SHA-256 ae09329225e321efa6204c8d4d5bf3b5ecef205954621d2e7b3fb7e15ad34c65). Resolves RFC 0002 section 8 question 5 for the deterministic-subset contract.

success_criterion.criterion: AVMP v2 must achieve total_oom <= min(fixed_dual_mr05.total_oom, fixed_dual_mr09.total_oom) on every workload in the default 270-cell sweep, with the v2 variant configured_total_bytes not exceeding any baseline configured_total_bytes.
success_criterion.note: CapacityError-branch placement (RFC 0002 section 4.2 wording, sub-PR 4) materially reduces OOMs on uniform_short and agentic_burst. mixed_long shows a small regression (+8.7) because the trigger fires inside heavy mixed_long cells but cannot find enough free SSM/KV to relieve pressure; rebalance overhead adds to total OOMs without recovery. Follow-up RFC: per-workload threshold tuning or batch-size scaling.
success_criterion.verdict: DID NOT PASS the strict success criterion (mixed_long: 558.7 > 550.0, +8.7 OOMs), BUT the pre-registered C1 stop-condition holds on uniform_short (7.0 < 7.3) and agentic_burst (220.7 < 227.3). Per the brief, C1 is a CONTINUE condition.
